title: Glass Onion
logline: Detective Benoit Blanc investigates the murder of tech billionaire Miles Bron's former business partner during a murder mystery game weekend on a Greek island, uncovering layers of deception among Bron's wealthy friends.
protagonist: Benoit Blanc and Helen Brand (Andi's twin sister)
antagonistOrConflict: Miles Bron, who murdered Andi Brand and Duke Cody to protect his stolen empire
tone: Satirical and witty with dark undertones, blending murder mystery conventions with sharp social commentary
themes: The corrupting nature of wealth and power
The hollow nature of tech disruption culture

actBreaks.description: Andi is shot and killed, ending the setup and launching the investigation.

validation.ruling: Scene #51 is the major revelation where Helen unmasks to Blanc, revealing she's not Andi but Andi's twin sister from Alabama, fundamentally shifting the story from a murder mystery party to a real murder investigation and changing everything we thought we knew.

actBreaks.description: Blanc completes his revelation of Miles as the murderer, shifting to the final confrontation.

validation.ruling: Scene #132 shows Blanc beginning to reveal how Duke was murdered through pineapple juice in his drink, marking the transition from investigation to full solution revelation as the truth about both murders is finally exposed.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a sophisticated non-linear structure that mirrors its central metaphor of peeling back layers. The first act establishes the puzzle box invitation and island gathering, building to Andi's apparent murder. The second act reveals Helen's true identity and uses extensive flashbacks to reconstruct both Andi's real murder and the events leading to Duke's poisoning. The structure cleverly withholds key information, allowing Blanc's deduction sequence to recontextualize everything we've seen. The screenplay uses a 'double reveal' approach - first showing us the murder mystery game, then revealing the real murders underneath.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing accelerates dramatically after scene 51 with Andi's death, then slows for the extended flashback sequence (scenes 54-89) that resets our understanding. The investigation phase (scenes 90-132) builds methodically through clue-gathering and deduction. The final act moves at breakneck speed through Blanc's revelation and the explosive climax, with very short scenes creating urgency.

incitingIncident.description: Helen tells Blanc about her sister Cassandra Brand, describing how Andi transformed from their shared childhood "rich bitch" character into a genuinely successful entrepreneur, and begins explaining the circumstances of Andi's death.

validation.ruling: Scene #16 is just a transition shot of water that doesn't shatter any status quo. Scene #53 is the true inciting incident where Helen reveals her identity to Blanc and explains she's Andi's twin sister seeking justice for what she believes was murder, fundamentally launching the main investigation plot.

midpoint.description: In a bathroom with a Matisse painting, Blanc praises Helen's snooping skills and explains that Lionel and Claire both have strong motives to protect Miles since they've bet everything on him, while Helen ticks off their motive boxes but doubts they could kill.

validation.ruling: Scene #75 is just exposition in a bathroom. Scene #74 at the pool is the true midpoint where Claire and Lionel privately reveal their devastating secrets - Claire signed off on a dangerous power plant and Lionel approved deadly Klear for space missions - establishing the life-or-death stakes that make them complicit and desperate enough to protect Miles at all costs.

climax.description: Miles confronts Helen about her destruction, but she reveals that his fuel burning the Mona Lisa will end his career and empire.
climax.sceneHeading: 149 EXT. THE GLASS ONION - MOMENTS LATER 149

validation.ruling: Scene #147 is the peak confrontation where Miles verbally attacks Helen after she destroys his compound, but Helen delivers the killing blow by revealing his Klear fuel destroyed the Mona Lisa, ruining him publicly, followed by all the disruptors turning against him and providing testimony that will convict him.

scenes.heading: 2 EXT. SUBURBAN HOUSE - DAY 2
scenes.summary: A courier delivers a package to Claire at her suburban house, with a title card establishing the date as May 13, 2020.

scenes.heading: 3 INT. DEBELLA HOME 3
scenes.summary: Claire brings the mysterious box inside where her husband Devon manages their kids, then rushes to prepare for a CNN interview in her makeshift home studio.

scenes.heading: 4 INT. AEROSPACE FACTORY 4
scenes.summary: In an empty aerospace factory, a worker moves through the space while a CNN interview about Miles Bron plays on an unattended TV.

scenes.heading: 5 INT. CONFERENCE ROOM 5
scenes.summary: Lionel sits alone in a conference room facing frustrated scientists on Zoom who tell him he should say no to Miles Bron's demands.

scenes.heading: 6 EXT. THE DAKOTA - MANHATTAN - DAY 6
scenes.summary: An establishing shot shows the Dakota apartment building in Manhattan on a clear day.

scenes.heading: 7 INT. APARTMENT 7
scenes.summary: Claire's TV interview plays in an apartment filled with partying people, while ex-model Birdie Jay lounges among admirers looking bored.

scenes.heading: 8 INT. DEBELLA HOME 8
scenes.summary: Claire calls Lionel on speaker to discuss the mysterious wooden box, with Devon helping examine it for opening mechanisms.

scenes.heading: 9 INT. THREE CAR GARAGE - DAY 9
scenes.summary: Duke broadcasts his YouTube show from his garage, defending his controversial comments about women with his girlfriend Whiskey making a brief appearance.

scenes.heading: 10 INT. DEBELLA HOME / CONFERENCE ROOM / APARTMENT 10
scenes.summary: The group continues their split-screen call as Lionel examines his box with a loupe and Birdie patches in Duke.

scenes.heading: 11 INT. DUKE'S KITCHEN - DAY 11
scenes.summary: Duke's mother helps him realize the box is a stereogram, and Lionel successfully opens his box by pressing a hidden button revealed through the Magic Eye pattern.
scenes.turningPointNote: The puzzle boxes are solved, revealing the invitations and setting the mystery weekend in motion

scenes.heading: 12 INT. DUKE'S KITCHEN 12
scenes.summary: Duke excitedly prepares to pack his spear fishing gear while Whiskey and his mother examine the opened invitation box.

scenes.heading: 13 INT. CONFERENCE ROOM 13
scenes.summary: Lionel leans back with his invitation and turns off the bank of monitors in his conference room.

scenes.heading: 14 INT. APARTMENT 14
scenes.summary: Birdie celebrates wildly, dancing around her party after receiving her invitation.

scenes.heading: 15 INT. GARAGE 15
scenes.summary: An unknown woman sits in a darkened garage staring at her unopened box, then returns with a hammer and smashes it to pieces.
scenes.turningPointNote: A mysterious character violently destroys their invitation, suggesting conflict and setting up Andi's antagonistic relationship with Miles

scenes.heading: 16 INT. BENOIT BLANC'S APARTMENT - DAY 16
scenes.summary: Benoit Blanc sits in his bathtub playing an online game with famous friends including Angela Lansbury and Stephen Sondheim, expressing confusion and disinterest in the game.

scenes.heading: 17 SHIMMERING WATER 17
scenes.summary: Opening title appears over shimmering water.

scenes.heading: 18 EXT. GRECIAN PORT TOWN - DAY 18
scenes.summary: At a Greek port, the invited guests gather on a jetty with their luggage, maintaining social distance with masks, while Blanc sits alone and ignored.

scenes.heading: 19 EXT. GRECIAN PORT TOWN STREETS - DAY 19
scenes.summary: Duke arrives dramatically on a motorcycle with Whiskey, who fires a gun into the air, joining the group at the jetty.

scenes.heading: 20 EXT. JETTY 20
scenes.summary: The group greets Duke and Whiskey, with Duke suspiciously questioning Blanc's presence and connection to Miles, until an efficient man arrives to transport them.

scenes.heading: 21 INT. BOAT - AT SEA - DAY 21
scenes.summary: On the boat, Blanc approaches Lionel who explains that Andi is Cassandra Brand, Miles's former business partner who was legally cut out of Alpha completely.
scenes.turningPointNote: Key exposition reveals Andi's identity and her contentious history with Miles, establishing major conflict

scenes.heading: 22 EXT. GREEK ISLAND - DAY 22
scenes.summary: Aerial shot shows the boat approaching a small Greek island with a single compound built on it.

scenes.heading: 23 EXT. BEACH - DAY 23
scenes.summary: As the boat approaches the beach, a glass dock with political art magically rises from the sea piece by piece.

scenes.heading: 24 INT. BOAT 24
scenes.summary: Lionel asks Captain Andino about the dock and the island's name, learning it's called 'Peet-cha-chite.'

scenes.heading: 25 EXT. BEACH 25
scenes.summary: Miles Bron sits on the beach playing guitar, greeting his arriving friends with warm hugs and showing off Paul McCartney's guitar.

scenes.heading: 26 EXT. VILLA - COMPOUND 26
scenes.summary: The group walks up to the compound and sees the main building - a massive glass orb that Miles calls his 'glass onion' representing past, present, and future.

scenes.heading: 27 INT. THE GLASS ONION - DAY 27
scenes.summary: Miles shows Blanc his office in the glass dome, including his car on the roof, then directly confronts Blanc about not inviting him to the island.
scenes.turningPointNote: Miles reveals he didn't invite Blanc, creating immediate tension and mystery about who did

scenes.heading: 28 EXT. POOL - DAY 28
scenes.summary: At the pool, the friends relax and banter while Miles serves drinks, with underlying tensions visible as Claire watches Andi disappear into the distance.

scenes.heading: 29 EXT. GROUNDS - CONTINUOUS 29
scenes.summary: Claire tells Lionel she's concerned about Andi's changed behavior and what she might be planning.

scenes.heading: 30 EXT. ZEN GARDEN - DAY 30
scenes.summary: Peg confronts Miles about a statement he wants Birdie to make regarding Bangladesh, pleading with him not to destroy Birdie's reputation.

scenes.heading: 31 EXT. GROUNDS - CONTINUOUS 31
scenes.summary: Duke spies on Miles and Whiskey kissing in a bedroom window, with Blanc secretly observing Duke's reaction of rage.
scenes.turningPointNote: Duke discovers Miles's affair with his girlfriend, creating a powerful motive for murder

scenes.heading: 32 EXT. THE GLASS ONION - EVENING 32
scenes.summary: The glass onion structure glows softly in the evening light as the hourly chime sounds.

scenes.heading: 33 INT. ATRIUM - EVENING 33
scenes.summary: Miles welcomes everyone for pre-murder drinks in the glass onion's main floor, serving personalized cocktails and explaining the murder mystery game setup.

scenes.heading: 34 INT. DINING AREA - MINUTES LATER 34
scenes.summary: During dinner, Miles explains the murder mystery game rules while the guests look uncomfortable, then Blanc solves the fake murder instantly by identifying Birdie as the killer.

scenes.heading: 35 INT. THE GLASS ONION - EVENING 35
scenes.summary: Miles angrily confronts Blanc in his office about solving the elaborate murder mystery too quickly, complaining about the expense and effort involved.

scenes.heading: 36 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 36
scenes.summary: The group drinks heavily in the lounge area while Duke checks his phone alerts, announces he's leaving in the morning, and Claire ignores calls from her husband.

scenes.heading: 37 INT. RADIO ROOM - CONTINUOUS 37
scenes.summary: Lionel discovers on the radio that the boat can't return until morning due to the malfunctioning Banksy dock being set to the wrong tide height.

scenes.heading: 38 INT. LOUNGE AREA 38
scenes.summary: Blanc suggests everyone retire safely to their rooms, but Miles notices Duke drank from Miles's personalized glass before collapsing, realizing the poison was meant for him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Duke's death reveals Miles was the intended target, transforming the fake murder game into a real murder investigation

scenes.heading: 39 EXT. THE GLASS ONION - NIGHT 39
scenes.summary: The entire complex goes dark as the lighthouse begins sweeping its beam across the grounds.

scenes.heading: 40 INT. ATRIUM - NIGHT 40
scenes.summary: In pitch darkness, the lighthouse beam reveals Miles clinging to Blanc's legs while everyone else scatters, with voices calling out in confusion.

scenes.heading: 41 INT. HALLWAYS - NIGHT 41
scenes.summary: Miles runs through dark hallways while Andi spots him from a distance, with Blanc's voice calling for Andi in the background.

scenes.heading: 42 INT. ATRIUM - NIGHT 42
scenes.summary: Peg finds herself alone in the dark atrium with only her phone's flashlight, calling out for the others who don't respond.

scenes.heading: 43 INT. HALLWAYS - NIGHT 43
scenes.summary: Miles moves slowly through dark hallways, barely breathing and listening for threats as shadows loom around him.

scenes.heading: 44 INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT 44
scenes.summary: Blanc enters the kitchen as a dark figure exits, discovering a missing knife from the butcher's block and seeing someone run past the window.

scenes.heading: 45 EXT. GROUNDS - NIGHT 45
scenes.summary: Blanc runs outside and collides with Andi, asking about Duke's gun and informing her that Duke is dead.

scenes.heading: 46 INT. HALL - NIGHT 46
scenes.summary: A gloved hand holds Duke's gun in the darkness, with a silhouette taking careful aim at Blanc and Andi outside the window.

scenes.heading: 47 EXT. GROUNDS - CONTINUOUS 47
scenes.summary: A gunshot shatters the window and Andi's body flies out of frame as if struck, while Blanc remains outside.
scenes.turningPointNote: Andi is shot and apparently killed, escalating the stakes to maximum as the real murderer strikes again

scenes.heading: 48 INT. THE GLASS ONION 48
scenes.summary: The gunshot echoes through the empty halls and rooms of the glass onion structure.

scenes.heading: 49 INT. ATRIUM 49
scenes.summary: The Mona Lisa's protective glass slides down as voices call out through the distant halls, with everyone trying to locate each other and Miles.

scenes.heading: 50 EXT. GROUNDS 50
scenes.summary: The lighthouse beam sweeps over Andi's crumpled body as Blanc stands above her, then the lights return and everyone appears in windows looking down at the scene.

scenes.heading: 51 INT. ATRIUM - NIGHT 51
scenes.summary: Blanc takes charge, ordering Peg to radio for boats while explaining that both murders and the entire weekend make perfect sense, though he stops short of revealing the full solution.
scenes.turningPointNote: Blanc declares he understands the entire mystery, setting up the final revelation phase

scenes.heading: 53 EXT. TERRACE - DAY 53
scenes.summary: On Blanc's terrace, Helen removes her mask and reveals she is not Andi but Helen Brand from Alabama, showing Blanc the shattered remains of a puzzle box.
scenes.turningPointNote: Major revelation that the woman we thought was Andi is actually her twin sister Helen, fundamentally shifting our understanding of the story

scenes.heading: 54 INT. MORGUE - FLASHBACK 54
scenes.summary: In a morgue flashback, Helen identifies her identical twin sister Andi's body on a stretcher after Andi's apparent suicide by carbon monoxide poisoning.

scenes.heading: 55 EXT. TERRACE 55
scenes.summary: Helen tells Blanc about her sister Cassandra Brand, describing how Andi transformed from their shared childhood "rich bitch" character into a genuinely successful entrepreneur, and begins explaining the circumstances of Andi's death.

scenes.heading: 56 SHIMMERING WATER 56
scenes.summary: A transition shot of shimmering water at night, sparkling like inky glass.

scenes.heading: 57 EXT. GRECIAN PORT TOWN - NIGHT 57
scenes.summary: An establishing shot pushing in on an adorable dock-side hotel lit up warm and glowing in a Grecian port town.

scenes.heading: 58 EXT. HOTEL TERRACE - NIGHT 58
scenes.summary: Helen, disguised as Andi, meets Blanc at a hotel terrace where he coaches her on how to behave tomorrow, telling her to arrive late, stay cold, and use Andi's "rich bitch" voice and posture.

scenes.heading: 59 INT. THE GLASS ONION BAR - FLASHBACK - NIGHT 59
scenes.summary: In flashback at the Glass Onion bar, young Andi is shown as the warm center of the group with Birdie, Duke, Claire, and Lionel, all broke and directionless before Andi found their potential.

scenes.heading: 60 EXT. HOTEL - NIGHT 60
scenes.summary: Blanc asks what Miles's leadership philosophy means.

scenes.heading: 61 INT. THE GLASS ONION BAR - FLASHBACK - NIGHT 61
scenes.summary: In flashback, the group huddles while Miles is at the bar, with Andi deciding to humor Miles's grandiose ideas about immortality despite Lionel's skepticism about his previous failed ventures.

scenes.heading: 62 EXT. HOTEL - NIGHT 62
scenes.summary: Helen explains how Miles brought everyone success based on a napkin idea he and Andi created, leading to Alpha's explosive growth and Miles's increasingly ambitious projects including a dangerous hydrogen fuel called Klear.

scenes.heading: 63 INT. BOARD ROOM - FLASHBACK - DAY 63
scenes.summary: In an Alpha boardroom flashback, Andi firmly refuses to sign a contract for Miles's Klear project, warning it could literally blow up the world and threatening to walk away from the company.

scenes.heading: 64 EXT. HOTEL - NIGHT 64
scenes.summary: Helen tells Blanc that Andi followed through on leaving the company, only to discover Miles's lawyers had structured contracts to cut her out completely, leading to her lawsuit over intellectual ownership.

scenes.heading: 65 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 65
scenes.summary: In a courtroom flashback, Claire, Duke, and Birdie all testify that Miles wrote the founding idea on the napkin, with Andi standing and furiously calling Claire a liar.

scenes.heading: 66 INT. ANDI'S LIVING ROOM - FLASHBACK - DAY 66
scenes.summary: In Andi's living room after losing the case, she stares dead-eyed at a Forbes story about Miles and his napkin, then goes into a destructive rage, throwing her iPad and destroying furniture.

scenes.heading: 67 EXT. HOTEL - NIGHT 67
scenes.summary: Blanc calls Miles's napkin story a bald-faced fabrication, and Helen agrees it was a damn lie that worked.

scenes.heading: 68 INT. ANDI'S LIVING ROOM - FLASHBACK - DAY 68
scenes.summary: Mid-destruction, Andi stops and finds her old copy of "The Innovator's Dilemma," from which the real napkin with her handwriting falls out.
scenes.turningPointNote: Discovery of the real napkin provides crucial evidence that could expose Miles's lies and restore Andi's reputation

scenes.heading: 69 EXT. HOTEL - NIGHT 69
scenes.summary: Blanc explains they need to find motives strong enough for murder and determine who had opportunity to kill Andi, while Helen draws a suspect grid with columns for motive and opportunity.

scenes.heading: 70 EXT. SEA - MORNING 70
scenes.summary: On the ferry to Bron's island, Helen stands seasick on the back deck while Duke and Birdie watch her with suspicion and hostility, telling her she shouldn't be there.

scenes.heading: 71 EXT. BEACH - DAY 71
scenes.summary: On the beach, Blanc ties his shoelace while quietly briefing Helen that Duke and Birdie seem suspicious of her, and instructs her to snoop while Miles explains Blanc's presence to the group.

scenes.heading: 72 INT. ANDI'S ROOM - DAY 72
scenes.summary: Helen puts her bag down in Andi's room and psychs herself up to start snooping.

scenes.heading: 73 EXT. GROUNDS 73
scenes.summary: Helen wanders the grounds in a bathing suit when Whiskey approaches, offers her hard kombucha, and they walk together with Helen complimenting Whiskey's Taurus necklace.

scenes.heading: 74 EXT. GROUNDS - CONTINUOUS 74
scenes.summary: Whiskey explains this is her second gathering with the group, complaining that Duke treats her like arm candy and she's considering distancing herself from his increasingly right-wing content for her political ambitions.

scenes.heading: 75 EXT. HERB GARDEN 75
scenes.summary: Helen emerges from the herb garden lost and frustrated, taking another pull from the kombucha when she hears a gunshot and spits out her drink.

scenes.heading: 76 EXT. POOL - CONTINUOUS 76
scenes.summary: At the pool, Duke has just fired his pistol while Lionel and Claire swim to the deep end for privacy, where Claire reveals she signed off on a power plant and Lionel admits he approved Klear for a manned space mission despite knowing it's dangerous.

scenes.heading: 77 INT. BATHROOM - LATER 77
scenes.summary: In a bathroom with a Matisse painting, Blanc praises Helen's snooping skills and explains that Lionel and Claire both have strong motives to protect Miles since they've bet everything on him, while Helen ticks off their motive boxes but doubts they could kill.

scenes.heading: 78 EXT. ANDI'S HOUSE - HYPOTHETICAL - EVENING 78
scenes.summary: In a hypothetical scenario, Duke knocks on Andi's front door with his motorcycle nearby, appearing contrite as if coming to apologize.

scenes.heading: 79 INT. ANDI'S KITCHEN - HYPOTHETICAL - NIGHT 79
scenes.summary: In hypothetical murder scenarios, Lionel slips powder into Andi's tea, Claire watches coldly as Andi's head sinks to the table, showing how any of them could have committed a gentle, non-violent murder.

scenes.heading: 80 INT. ANDI'S GARAGE - HYPOTHETICAL - EVENING 80
scenes.summary: In another hypothetical scenario, Birdie turns on a car engine with Andi slumped in the passenger seat, demonstrating how the murder could have been staged as suicide.

scenes.heading: 81 INT. BATHROOM 81
scenes.summary: Back in the bathroom, Blanc realizes Helen has been drinking hard kombucha thinking it was health food, and she drunkenly insists they need to push the suspects to determine opportunity.

scenes.heading: 82 EXT. POOL 82
scenes.summary: At the pool, Helen listens to Miles's speech about disruption while getting increasingly drunk, then claps and draws everyone's attention as Blanc watches with concern.

scenes.heading: 83 EXT. GROUNDS - AFTERNOON 83
scenes.summary: Helen bursts away from the pool area, drunk and hyped up, when Claire chases after her and confronts her, asking what she's doing and mentioning an email Andi supposedly sent.

scenes.heading: 84 EXT. ANDI'S HOUSE - FLASHBACK - EVENING 84
scenes.summary: In flashback, Claire describes how she, Lionel, and Duke all went to Andi's house that evening, finding the lights out and no answer despite pounding on the door and calling for her.

scenes.heading: 85 EXT. GROUNDS 85
scenes.summary: Claire explains they waited an hour before leaving, with Birdie checking later and finding the same situation, while Helen drunkenly tries to keep the timeline straight and asks for clarification.

scenes.heading: 86 EXT. GARDEN 86
scenes.summary: Helen stumbles away from the confrontation, disoriented by flashing red lights and a soothing robot voice warning about the smokeless garden, until Blanc appears from the foliage looking panicked.

scenes.heading: 87 INT. GYM - DAY 87
scenes.summary: In the gym, Helen reports everything Claire said while pounding Gatorade, and she and Blanc determine that all four suspects had both motive and opportunity, filling out their entire suspect grid.

scenes.heading: 88 EXT. GROUNDS 88
scenes.summary: Duke creeps through foliage to spy on Miles and Whiskey making out through a window, while Helen and Blanc watch him from a distance until Helen snaps a twig trying to get closer.

scenes.heading: 89 EXT. BIRDIE'S VILLA - AFTERNOON 89
scenes.summary: When Peg storms away from Birdie's villa, Helen dashes in to grab Birdie's bag and retrieves her phone, checking that it's still recording.

scenes.heading: 90 INT. ANDI'S VILLA - MINUTES LATER 90
scenes.summary: Helen and Blanc listen to a recording on the phone of Peg demanding answers from Birdie about what Miles meant by "it's her only chance."

scenes.heading: 91 INT. BIRDIE'S VILLA - FLASHBACK - AFTERNOON 91
scenes.summary: In flashback, the recording reveals Birdie crying as she shows Peg her secret phone with an email from two years ago warning about a sweatshop factory, which Birdie ignored, and now Miles is forcing her to take responsibility to protect him.

scenes.heading: 92 INT. ANDI'S VILLA 92
scenes.summary: Helen stops the recording and glumly fills in the final motive boxes for Birdie and Duke, completing their suspect grid, while Blanc theorizes that the killer would have brought the envelope to Miles to show what they did for him.

scenes.heading: 93 INT. ATRIUM - NIGHT 93
scenes.summary: In the atrium at night, Miles offers Helen a whiskey soda but Blanc signals her to avoid it, so she hides it behind a statue while the Mona Lisa's protective glass slides down.

scenes.heading: 94 EXT. GROUNDS - NIGHT 94
scenes.summary: Helen sprints through the grounds toward the suspects' villas as soon as she's out of sight.

scenes.heading: 95 INT. CLAIRE'S VILLA - NIGHT 95
scenes.summary: Helen tears apart Claire's villa, searching through clothes, electronics, and personal items, leaving the room looking like a tornado hit it, but finds no envelope.

scenes.heading: 96 EXT. GROUNDS - NIGHT 96
scenes.summary: Helen sprints through the grounds with the glowing glass onion structure looming above her as time ticks away.

scenes.heading: 97 INT. ATRIUM - NIGHT 97
scenes.summary: Back in the atrium, Miles cheers as Birdie spins in her rainbow dress.

scenes.heading: 98 INT. BIRDIE'S VILLA - NIGHT 98
scenes.summary: Helen searches through Birdie's villa, finding designer clothes, prescription pills, and a dog-eared copy of Ayn Rand's "The Fountainhead" when her phone buzzes.

scenes.heading: 99 INT. ATRIUM - NIGHT 99
scenes.summary: In the atrium, Duke grasps his throat after downing his drink.
scenes.turningPointNote: Duke's apparent poisoning creates a new murder in real time, escalating the stakes dramatically

scenes.heading: 100 INT. LIONEL'S VILLA 100
scenes.summary: Helen searches Lionel's villa, finding stylish clothes, computer equipment, condoms, and LSD micro-doses, ignoring another buzz from her phone.

scenes.heading: 101 INT. ATRIUM - NIGHT 101
scenes.summary: In the aftermath of Duke's death, Whiskey staggers away from the group sobbing and runs off into the night.

scenes.heading: 102 INT. DEROL'S VILLA - NIGHT 102
scenes.summary: Helen bursts into Derol's cluttered villa where he sits in an E-Z Boy chair watching TV, greeting her casually before she immediately runs out, with him asking if she wants to hang out.

scenes.heading: 103 INT. DUKE'S VILLA - NIGHT 103
scenes.summary: Helen discovers through dozens of messages that Cassandra Brand's death has been reported as suicide, then searches Duke's villa for the envelope but finds nothing.

scenes.heading: 104 EXT. GROUNDS - NIGHT 104
scenes.summary: Helen runs desperately toward the glass onion in the darkness, screaming in frustration.

scenes.heading: 105 INT. HALLWAYS - NIGHT 105
scenes.summary: Helen encounters Miles in the dark hallways before hearing Blanc's voice and running toward it, while Lionel emerges after she leaves.

scenes.heading: 106 EXT. THE GLASS ONION - NIGHT 106
scenes.summary: Helen reunites with Blanc outside the glass onion, learning Duke is dead and that they must search one final room for the envelope.

scenes.heading: 107 INT. HALLWAYS - NIGHT 107
scenes.summary: Helen sneaks through the hallways while Blanc's voice echoes from below discussing Duke, Andi, and the weekend's events.

scenes.heading: 108 INT. LOUNGE AREA - CONTINUOUS 108
scenes.summary: Blanc gathers the suspects and begins explaining the case, stating only one person can reveal who killed Cassandra Brand.

scenes.heading: 109 INT. THE GLASS ONION - NIGHT 109
scenes.summary: Helen searches the glass office space frantically while Blanc's voice continues explaining the glass onion metaphor.

scenes.heading: 110 INT. LOUNGE AREA 110
scenes.summary: Blanc explains how the case confounded him by expecting complexity when the truth was simple and obvious.

scenes.heading: 111 INT. THE GLASS ONION 111
scenes.summary: Helen discovers the framed napkin and photo of Andi and Miles, pulling it from the wall to reveal the red envelope falling out.
scenes.turningPointNote: Helen finds the crucial evidence that will expose Miles

scenes.heading: 112 INT. LOUNGE AREA 112
scenes.summary: Blanc reveals that Miles's use of the non-word 'embreathiate' was the key clue that exposed him.

scenes.heading: 113 EXT. BEACH - FLASHBACK - DAY 113
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles using the made-up word 'embreathiate' when the group first arrived.

scenes.heading: 114 INT. LOUNGE AREA 114
scenes.summary: Blanc explains that 'embreathiate' is not a real word and 'reclamation' is the wrong word for Miles's usage.

scenes.heading: 115 EXT. THE GLASS ONION - FLASHBACK - DAY 115
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles incorrectly using 'reclamation' when describing his accomplishments.

scenes.heading: 116 INT. LOUNGE AREA 116
scenes.summary: Blanc continues his explanation of Miles's pattern of errors.

scenes.heading: 117 INT. DISPLAY GARAGE - FLASHBACK - DAY 117
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles using 'predefinite' instead of the correct word when confronting Blanc.

scenes.heading: 118 INT. LOUNGE AREA 118
scenes.summary: Blanc describes Miles's speech as filled with malapropisms.

scenes.heading: 119 EXT. POOL - FLASHBACK - DAY 119
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles using 'infraction point' incorrectly while telling a story.

scenes.heading: 120 INT. LOUNGE AREA 120
scenes.summary: Blanc continues cataloging Miles's factual errors.

scenes.heading: 121 INT. DINING AREA - FLASHBACK - EVENING 121
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles incorrectly identifying the surrounding water as the Ionian Sea.

scenes.heading: 122 INT. LOUNGE AREA 122
scenes.summary: Blanc reveals the central truth that Miles Bron is an idiot, not a complicated genius.
scenes.turningPointNote: Blanc exposes the fundamental misunderstanding about Miles's intelligence

scenes.heading: 123 EXT. ANDI'S HOUSE - FLASHBACK - EVENING 123
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Duke nearly being hit by Miles's distinctive blue Porsche leaving Andi's house.

scenes.heading: 124 EXT. POOL - FLASHBACK - DAY 124
scenes.summary: Flashback reveals Duke clearly stating Miles almost hit him with the Porsche 'that night at Andi's.'

scenes.heading: 125 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 125
scenes.summary: Blanc establishes that Miles was in New York two weeks ago for Whiskey's birthday, not Greece as claimed.

scenes.heading: 126 INT. AEROSPACE FACTORY - FLASHBACK - AFTERNOON 126
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Lionel faxing Andi's threatening email to Miles.

scenes.heading: 127 INT. NEW YORK PENTHOUSE - FLASHBACK - AFTERNOON 127
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles receiving the fax in New York and realizing the threat to his empire.

scenes.heading: 128 EXT. ANDI'S HOUSE ROAD - FLASHBACK - LATE AFTERNOON 128
scenes.summary: Blanc describes Miles driving his signature Porsche to the crime scene.

scenes.heading: 129 INT. ATRIUM 129
scenes.summary: Blanc states Miles drove his distinctive Porsche to commit the crime.

scenes.heading: 130 EXT. ANDI'S HOUSE - FLASHBACK - LATE AFTERNOON 130
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles's car parked outside Andi's house.

scenes.heading: 131 INT. ANDI'S KITCHEN - FLASHBACK - LATE AFTERNOON 131
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles poisoning Andi's tea while she watches, then becoming distracted by a spider as she loses consciousness.

scenes.heading: 132 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 132
scenes.summary: Blanc reveals that Duke knew Miles was at Andi's but didn't know she was dead until seeing the news alert.

scenes.heading: 133 INT. LOUNGE AREA - FLASHBACK - EARLIER THAT NIGHT 133
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Duke revealing Andi's death to Miles and demanding a position at Alpha News in exchange for silence.

scenes.heading: 134 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 134
scenes.summary: Helen asks if Miles keeps poison in his tooth while Blanc begins explaining the murder method involving pineapple juice.

scenes.heading: 135 INT. LOUNGE AREA - FLASHBACK - NIGHT 135
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles secretly adding pineapple juice to Duke's whiskey at the bar cart.

scenes.heading: 136 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 136
scenes.summary: Blanc explains that Miles killed Duke with pineapple juice, exploiting his deadly allergy, while the group realizes Helen's true identity.

scenes.heading: 137 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT - FLASHBACK 137
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles calculating his next move during the blackout.

scenes.heading: 138 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 138
scenes.summary: Blanc continues explaining Miles's opportunity during the blackout.

scenes.heading: 139 INT. LOUNGE AREA - FLASHBACK - NIGHT 139
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles grabbing Duke's gun during the dark confusion.

scenes.heading: 140 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 140
scenes.summary: Blanc begins to realize something familiar about the murder setup.

scenes.heading: 141 INT. HALLWAYS - FLASHBACK - NIGHT 141
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles moving through dark hallways with Duke's phone and gun.

scenes.heading: 142 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 142
scenes.summary: Blanc has a sudden realization about why the murder scenario sounds familiar.

scenes.heading: 143 INT. THE GLASS ONION - FLASHBACK - EVENING 143
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Blanc earlier explaining to Miles how gathering enemies on an island with murder in mind is like putting a loaded gun on a table.

scenes.heading: 144 INT. LOUNGE AREA 144
scenes.summary: Blanc realizes Miles stole the entire murder plan from his own earlier explanation.

scenes.heading: 145 INT. HALLWAYS - FLASHBACK - NIGHT 145
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles shooting at Helen through the window, shattering the glass.

scenes.heading: 146 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 146
scenes.summary: Helen confronts Miles with the original napkin proving Andi's authorship, but Miles burns it with his lighter and destroys the Mona Lisa's protective glass.
scenes.turningPointNote: Miles destroys the crucial evidence and the priceless painting in one devastating act

scenes.heading: 147 EXT. THE GLASS ONION - NIGHT 147
scenes.summary: The glass onion and all the villas explode in a chain reaction of fire and destruction while Blanc and Derol watch from lawn chairs.

scenes.heading: 148 INT. ATRIUM - NIGHT 148
scenes.summary: Everyone emerges dazed from the burning atrium as Miles's Porsche crashes down from above, and Helen runs toward the Mona Lisa while others try to stop her.

scenes.heading: 149 EXT. THE GLASS ONION - MOMENTS LATER 149
scenes.summary: Miles confronts Helen about her destruction, but she reveals that his fuel burning the Mona Lisa will end his career and empire.
scenes.turningPointNote: Helen's final gambit destroys Miles's reputation and future through the public destruction of the world's most famous painting

scenes.heading: 150 EXT. BEACH - NIGHT 150
scenes.summary: Helen and Blanc sit on the beach watching police boats approach, with Helen appearing satisfied as they prepare to leave.

structuralWarnings.text: Act 3 is only 9% of the script — structural placement suspect
2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
